soap:
RE-REVIEW · lancedb/lancedb#3584 — feat: add polars dataframe integration
reviewed 06259a3e → 64e1d8e6  (13 commits, 5 PR-own files; 46 base-merge files excluded)
domain: rust/lancedb

================================================================
SUBJECTIVE
================================================================
Re_review lane: author pushed since my last look. PR adds a first-class
Polars DataFrame integration to the Rust crate — `polars::frame::DataFrame`
now implements the internal `Scannable` trait so it can be passed directly to
`create_table` / `add`, and results round-trip back out via the existing
`IntoPolars`. Alongside this, the PR removes the older public `IntoArrow` /
`IntoArrowStream` conversion traits in favor of `Scannable`.

My reconstructed conditions ledger from prior posted issue(blocking) comments
is EMPTY (0 conditions). There were no outstanding blocking conditions from my
last review to verify against this delta.

================================================================
OBJECTIVE  (verified against the commit-anchored delta patch, not GitHub claims)
================================================================
Delta = 06259a3e...64e1d8e6, scoped to the PR's own changed files:

  rust/lancedb/src/data/scannable.rs   +93 -0   (new Scannable impl + 2 tests)
  rust/lancedb/src/arrow.rs            +2 -47   (removes IntoArrow/IntoArrowStream)
  rust/lancedb/examples/polars.rs      +47 -0   (new, feature-gated example)
  rust/lancedb/Cargo.toml              +15 -3   (version bump, deps, example entry)
  nodejs/src/merge.rs                  +4 -6    (callsite updated for trait removal)

Ground-truth checks performed:
  - Scannable trait signature at head (scannable.rs:28-55) — schema()->SchemaRef,
    scan_as_stream(&mut self)->SendableRecordBatchStream, num_rows()->Option<usize>,
    rescannable()->bool. New `impl Scannable for DataFrame` matches exactly and
    reuses the same SimpleRecordBatchStream / futures::stream patterns as the
    existing impls (imports present: once, stream::iter, SimpleRecordBatchStream).
  - Referenced symbols exist at head: PolarsDataFrameRecordBatchReader struct +
    Iterator + RecordBatchReader impls (arrow.rs:145-200), IntoPolars trait
    (arrow.rs:203), polars_arrow_convertors module present.
  - DANGLING-REFERENCE SWEEP for the removed traits: fetched all 120 .rs files
    at head sha 64e1d8e6 and grepped for `IntoArrow` (and case-insensitive
    `into_arrow`). ZERO matches in any file, including arrow.rs itself. The
    nodejs callsite (merge.rs) is the only consumer and was updated. => no
    build break from the trait removal.
  - Tests present and meaningful: test_dataframe_scannable_round_trip (create +
    add + read-back, asserts height*2 and schema equality) and
    test_dataframe_scannable_rescannable (scans twice, asserts row counts).

================================================================
ASSESSMENT
================================================================

CONDITIONS LEDGER (0/0 met)
  (no prior blocking conditions to reconcile — ledger empty)

FRESH-EYES DELTA
  No new blocking concerns. The new code is internally consistent, gated behind
  the `polars` feature, and covered by round-trip + rescannable tests. Three
  non-blocking observations:

  1. note (non-blocking) — Public API break. Removing the public `IntoArrow`
     and `IntoArrowStream` traits is a breaking change for any downstream crate
     that imported them. Confirmed clean inside this repo (zero remaining refs),
     but external users are affected. Acceptable for a 0.31.0-beta crate and
     arguably the point of the refactor (consolidating on Scannable); flag so
     it lands in release notes / changelog.

  2. note (non-blocking) — `<DataFrame as Scannable>::scan_as_stream`
     (scannable.rs) materializes ALL record batches into a Vec before turning
     them into a stream, so it is not lazy. Defensible: a Polars DataFrame is
     already fully resident in memory, so there is no extra peak-memory cost
     vs. the source. Not a concern, just noting it isn't streaming.

  3. note (non-blocking) — `<DataFrame as Scannable>::schema` uses `.expect(..)`
     and will panic if Polars->Arrow schema conversion fails. This is forced by
     the trait method returning a bare SchemaRef (not a Result), and matches
     how the other Scannable impls behave, so it is consistent with the
     existing contract rather than a new wart.

  The Cargo.toml additions of `urlencoding` (to the `remote` feature) and the
  `tokio` `sync`/dev-dep features plus the version bump look like base-branch
  merge churn unrelated to Polars; the Polars-specific wiring (the `[[example]]
  name = "polars"` entry with required-features) is correct.

================================================================
PLAN / RECOMMENDATION
================================================================
RECOMMENDATION: approve
  Drafted blocking comments: 0
  Confidence: high. 0 outstanding conditions; the only structural risk (a
  dangling reference from the trait removal) was checked exhaustively across all
  120 .rs files at head and is clean; new impl matches the trait and is tested.
  What would make this wrong: a downstream/off-repo consumer of the removed
  IntoArrow traits that I cannot see from this repo's tree, or a CI failure on
  the `polars` feature build I did not execute locally (I verified symbols and
  signatures by inspection at head, not by compiling).
